sections.paragraph:
a root unused in Kal; kindred to the root חָמַר (compare חָמַם, חוּם).

(1) to grow hot, to become warm, to glow; see Niphal No. 1 (Talmud כומר, a warming).

(2) to be burned, scorched (see Niphal No. 2); hence to be black, dark, obscure, like the Syriac ܟܡܰܪ to be sad, sorrowful. Aphel, to go about in black, i.e. mourning. Compare כִּמְדִירִים כֹּמֶר.

Niphal-

(1) to be warm, to glow, used of love towards any one; followed by עַל 1Ki 3:26, and אֶל Gen 43:20 to be moved, spoken of pity, Hos 11:8.

(2) to be scorched; Lam 5:10, “our skin is scorched as in an oven from the burning heat of of the famine.”

II.

כמר

i.q. כָּבַר to plait, to braid; whence מַכְמֹור מִכְמָר, מִכְמֶרֶת a net.
